description: Detect error rate spikes in application logs. Use this when investigating service errors or outages. Returns error counts bucketed by minute for the specified service and time range.

configuration.query: FROM ic-logs | WHERE @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime AND service.name == ?serviceName AND log.level == "error" | STATS error_count = COUNT(*) BY ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 1 minute) | SORT ts_bucket

serviceName.description: Name of the service to investigate

description: Follow a distributed trace across all services. Use this to understand the full request path and find where failures originated. Returns all log entries for a trace ID sorted by timestamp.

configuration.query: FROM ic-logs | WHERE trace.id == ?traceId | SORT @timestamp | LIMIT 100

traceId.description: The trace ID to follow across services

description: Find when an error type first appeared in a service. Use this to pinpoint the exact moment an issue started. Returns the earliest timestamp and context for the error.

configuration.query: FROM ic-logs | WHERE service.name == ?serviceName AND log.level == "error" AND @timestamp >= ?startTime | SORT @timestamp | LIMIT 5

description: Detect CPU and latency anomalies for a host using ES|QL INLINE STATS for single-query outlier detection. Flags 5-minute buckets where max CPU exceeds 1.5x overall average or latency exceeds 2x average.

configuration.query: FROM ic-metrics | WHERE host.name == ?hostName AND @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime | INLINE STATS overall_avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), overall_avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms) | STATS avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), max_cpu = MAX(system.cpu.total.pct), avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms), overall_avg_cpu = VALUES(overall_avg_cpu), overall_avg_latency = VALUES(overall_avg_latency) BY ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes) | EVAL cpu_anomaly = max_cpu > overall_avg_cpu * 1.5, latency_anomaly = avg_latency > overall_avg_latency * 2 | SORT ts_bucket

description: Correlate deployment events with metric anomalies. Use this to determine if a deployment caused a performance regression. Shows host metrics by 5-minute buckets for the specified service.

configuration.query: FROM ic-metrics | WHERE @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime AND service.name == ?serviceName | STATS avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms) BY host.name, ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes) | SORT ts_bucket

description: Query security alerts for a specific time range. Use this when investigating potential security incidents. Returns alert counts grouped by rule name, severity, and 5-minute time buckets.

configuration.query: FROM ic-security-alerts | WHERE @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime | STATS alert_count = COUNT(*) BY alert.rule_name, alert.severity, ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes) | SORT ts_bucket

description: Investigate a suspicious IP address across security alerts. Use this to understand the full activity pattern of a potential attacker. Returns event counts bucketed by 5-minute intervals for the specified source IP.

configuration.query: FROM ic-security-alerts | WHERE source.ip == ?suspiciousIp AND @timestamp >= ?startTime | STATS attempt_count = COUNT(*) BY ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes) | SORT ts_bucket

description: List recent deployments for a service. Use this to check if a recent deployment might have caused the incident. Returns deployment history with versions, authors, and timestamps.

configuration.query: FROM ic-deployments | WHERE deployment.service == ?serviceName AND @timestamp >= ?startTime | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 10

description: Search past resolved incidents using semantic similarity on root cause analysis (semantic_text field). Returns incidents with similar failure patterns, remediation steps, and involved agents. Useful for finding historical precedents.

configuration.query: FROM ic-incidents | WHERE incident.status == "resolved" | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 10

description: Health overview of all services with ES|QL INLINE STATS fleet-wide comparison. Shows per-service CPU, latency (avg/p99), request counts, and flags services performing worse than 1.5x fleet average.

configuration.query: FROM ic-metrics | WHERE @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime | INLINE STATS fleet_avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), fleet_avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms) | STATS avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), max_cpu = MAX(system.cpu.total.pct), avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms), p99_latency = PERCENTILE(http.response.latency_ms, 99), total_requests = SUM(http.request.count), fleet_avg_cpu = VALUES(fleet_avg_cpu), fleet_avg_latency = VALUES(fleet_avg_latency) BY service.name | EVAL cpu_above_fleet = avg_cpu > fleet_avg_cpu * 1.5, latency_above_fleet = avg_latency > fleet_avg_latency * 2 | SORT avg_latency DESC

description: Search threat intelligence for a suspicious IP address using semantic search. Queries the threat intel knowledge base to find known threat actors, attack patterns, and CVEs associated with an IP. Uses semantic_text for intelligent matching against threat descriptions.

configuration.query: FROM ic-threat-intel | WHERE indicator.ip == ?suspiciousIp | KEEP indicator.ip, indicator.type, threat.description, threat.severity, threat.actor, cve.id, last_seen | LIMIT 10

suspiciousIp.description: IP address to check against threat intelligence database

description: ES|QL LOOKUP JOIN: Correlate infrastructure metrics with deployment events by service. Shows CPU, latency (avg/max/p99) per deployment version per 5-minute bucket with deployment author and change description.

configuration.query: FROM ic-metrics | WHERE @timestamp >= ?startTime AND @timestamp <= ?endTime AND service.name == ?serviceName | EVAL deployment_service = service.name | LOOKUP JOIN ic-deployments-lookup ON deployment_service | STATS avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct), max_cpu = MAX(system.cpu.total.pct), avg_latency = AVG(http.response.latency_ms), max_latency = MAX(http.response.latency_ms), p99_latency = PERCENTILE(http.response.latency_ms, 99) BY deployment.version, deployment.author, deployment.changes, ts_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes) | SORT ts_bucket

serviceName.description: Service name to correlate deployments with metrics
